data.content: The build has two issues: missing `System` namespace (implicit usings not enabled) and `ThrowsExactly` not available in MSTest 3.6.3. Let me fix both by enabling implicit usings and upgrading MSTest:

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-69ee7a9dca34426ba4d4252fdb645928 && dotnet package search MSTest.TestFramework --take 1 2>&1 | grep -A2 "MSTest.TestFramework"

data.reasoningText:
The issue is that `Assert.ThrowsExactly` is not available in MSTest 3.6.3 - it was introduced in MSTest v4. Also, `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` needs `using System;` or the project needs implicit usings. Let me check if implicit usings are enabled and what version of MSTest has `ThrowsExactly`.

Looking at the errors:
1. `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` not found - needs `using System;` 
2. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` not found - this is MSTest v4 API

Wait, the project is net8.0 without `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>`. 

The original test used `[ExpectedException(typeof(ArgumentOutOfRangeException))]` which worked, so either there was implicit usings or a global using statement. Since .NET 6+ enables implicit usings by default with the SDK, it should be active here, but the error suggests it's not being recognized.

Wait, I'm also noticing the error mentions `Assert.ThrowsExactly` doesn't exist either. That method was added in MSTest v4, but if the project is using MSTest 3.x, the correct method would be `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` instead. Let me verify what version of MSTest is actually being used. I need to verify the exact version where `Assert.ThrowsExactly` was introduced—it might be 3.7.0 or possibly a v4 feature. The most practical solution is to upgrade MSTest to whichever version supports it, since that's what the user is asking for. I should explicitly enable `ImplicitUsings` in the project file to ensure `System` is available, and I'm wondering if the SDK version might be causing compatibility issues with net8.0. Let me try restoring the project and checking whether `ThrowsExactly` is actually available in the current MSTest version. I'm realizing `ThrowsExactly` is actually an MSTest v4 feature, so I need to upgrade to the latest version rather than trying to work around it with an older release. The most straightforward fix is enabling implicit usings in the project file and then upgrading the MSTest packages to v4.x to get access to `ThrowsExactly`. the latest MSTest and enabling implicit usings to clean things up. I'll upgrade to the newest stable version and check what assertion methods are actually available in that release.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="4.2.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="4.2.2" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
MSTest 4.2.2 is available. Let me upgrade to that version and also enable implicit usings. Actually, the user asked for "current MSTest best practices" so upgrading to MSTest 4.x makes sense. But MSTest v4 has breaking changes. Let me just use 3.7+ which should have ThrowsExactly. Actually wait - the build error said `Assert.ThrowsExactly` is not in `Assert`. Let me check if it was introduced in 3.7 or 4.0.

Looking at the error and the MSTest v3-to-v4 migration skill description: "Assert.ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly" is listed as a v4 change. 

So `ThrowsExactly` is an MSTest v4 API. Since the user explicitly asked for `ThrowsExactly` and current best practices, I should upgrade to MSTest 4.2.2. The breaking changes shouldn't be an issue with the sealed class and modernized code. For MSTest 4.x, I'll use the `MSTest` meta-package or `MSTest.Sdk` for the package references.

data.content:
All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of the modernizations applied:

| Anti-pattern | Fix |
|---|---|
| Non-sealed class | `public sealed class` |
| Nullable `TestContext?` | `TestContext = null!` |
| `[TestInitialize]` for simple setup | Inline field initializer |
| Vague `Test1` name | Descriptive `Method_Scenario_Expected` naming |
| Swapped `Assert.AreEqual` args | Correct order: `(expected, actual)` |
| `[ExpectedException]` attribute | `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` |
| Hard cast + `Assert.IsTrue` | `Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>()` + `StringAssert.Contains` |
| Manual loop test cases | `[DataRow]` parameterized tests |

Also upgraded MSTest from 3.6.3 → 4.2.2 to get access to `Assert.ThrowsExactly` and enabled `ImplicitUsings`.
